revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 風間神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[長野県]][[長野市]]大字[[風間 (長野市)|風間]]779 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=風間神社}} |祭神 = [[伊勢津彦|伊勢津彦命]]
[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月1日]]・[[10月2日|2日]]（秋季例大祭） |神事 = }} '''風間神社'''（かざまじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]][[風間 (長野市)|風間]]にある[[神社]]。 [[式内社]]に比定される。 == 概要 == [[長野駅]]から南東約3kmほど、[[長野県道34号長野菅平線|県道]]からやや入った住宅地に鎮座する。 『[[日本書紀]]』では[[596年]]（[[推古天皇]]5年）「竜田風神信濃須坂・水内社を祀らしむ」、[[860年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年）「飄間神に叙位」との記述がある。これらが風間神社を指すと考えられる[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html 風間神社] - 長野県神社庁。[[977年]]（[[天元 (日本)|天元]]2年）には[[諏訪氏]]の庶流・矢島忠直が庄司として派遣され風間姓を名乗り、これが各地の[[風間氏]]の発祥となっている。 神社に伝わる「太々神楽獅子舞」は[[1822年]]（[[文政]]5年）から続く、歌詞がなく太刀を持つ雄獅子であり、[[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）に長野市[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html 風間神社太々神楽獅子舞] - 長野市文化財データベース。 == 歴史 == * [[596年]]（[[推古天皇]]5年）[[8月]] - [[日本書紀]]に記述が見える。 * [[860年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年）[[2月]] - 日本書紀に記述が見える。 * [[977年]]（[[天元 (日本)|天元]]2年） - [[諏訪氏]]の庶流・矢島忠直が庄司として派遣され、[[風間氏]]を名乗る。 * [[1697年]]（[[元禄]]10年） - 諏訪社から風間大明神に改称[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127　風間神社　〜式内社に改名した風神〜] - 週刊長野。 * [[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）[[8月1日]] - 「風間神社太々神楽獅子舞」が長野市[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定される。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]]・[[長野電鉄]][[長野駅]]から[[アルピコ交通]]（[[川中島バス]]）'''46'''系統に乗車、「風間」下車徒歩2分 == 周辺 == * 西風間公民館 * [[長野市役所]] 大豆島支所 * [[長野市立大豆島小学校]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[風間氏]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html 風間神社] - 長野県神社庁 * [http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ 風間神社太々神楽保存会] {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かさましんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野市の建築物]] [[Category:長野市の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]]

revision.comment: 860年記事の修正（日本書紀は720年成立）。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 風間神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[長野県]][[長野市]]大字[[風間 (長野市)|風間]]779 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=風間神社}} |祭神 = [[伊勢津彦|伊勢津彦命]]
[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月1日]]・[[10月2日|2日]]（秋季例大祭） |神事 = }} '''風間神社'''（かざまじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]][[風間 (長野市)|風間]]にある[[神社]]。 [[式内社]]に比定される。 == 概要 == [[長野駅]]から南東約3kmほど、[[長野県道34号長野菅平線|県道]]からやや入った住宅地に鎮座する。 『[[日本書紀]]』では[[596年]]（[[推古天皇]]5年）「竜田風神信濃須坂・水内社を祀らしむ」、[[860年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年）「飄別神に叙位」との記述がある。これらが風間神社を指すと考えられる[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html 風間神社] - 長野県神社庁。[[977年]]（[[天元 (日本)|天元]]2年）には[[諏訪氏]]の庶流・矢島忠直が庄司として派遣され風間姓を名乗り、これが各地の[[風間氏]]の発祥となっている。 神社に伝わる「太々神楽獅子舞」は[[1822年]]（[[文政]]5年）から続く、歌詞がなく太刀を持つ雄獅子であり、[[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）に長野市[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html 風間神社太々神楽獅子舞] - 長野市文化財データベース。 == 歴史 == * [[596年]]（[[推古天皇]]5年）[[8月]] - [[日本書紀]]に記述が見える。 * [[860年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年）[[2月]] - [[日本三代実録]]に記述が見える。 * [[977年]]（[[天元 (日本)|天元]]2年） - [[諏訪氏]]の庶流・矢島忠直が庄司として派遣され、[[風間氏]]を名乗る。 * [[1697年]]（[[元禄]]10年） - 諏訪社から風間大明神に改称[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127　風間神社　〜式内社に改名した風神〜] - 週刊長野。 * [[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）[[8月1日]] - 「風間神社太々神楽獅子舞」が長野市[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定される。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]]・[[長野電鉄]][[長野駅]]から[[アルピコ交通]]（[[川中島バス]]）'''46'''系統に乗車、「風間」下車徒歩2分 == 周辺 == * 西風間公民館 * [[長野市役所]] 大豆島支所 * [[長野市立大豆島小学校]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[風間氏]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html 風間神社] - 長野県神社庁 * [http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ 風間神社太々神楽保存会] {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かさましんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野市の建築物]] [[Category:長野市の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 風間神社 |画像 = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|風間神社（長野県長野市風間）]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[長野市]]大字[[風間 (長野市)|風間]]779 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=風間神社}} |祭神 = [[伊勢津彦|伊勢津彦命]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月1日]]・[[10月2日|2日]]（秋季例大祭） |神事 = }} '''風間神社'''（かざまじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]][[風間 (長野市)|風間]]にある[[神社]]。 [[式内社]]に比定される。 == 概要 == [[長野駅]]から南東約3kmほど、[[長野県道34号長野菅平線|県道]]からやや入った住宅地に鎮座する。 『[[日本書紀]]』では[[596年]]（[[推古天皇]]5年）「竜田風神信濃須坂・水内社を祀らしむ」、[[860年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年）「飄別神に叙位」との記述がある。これらが風間神社を指すと考えられる[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html 風間神社] - 長野県神社庁。[[979年]]（[[天元 (日本)|天元]]2年）には[[諏訪氏]]の庶流・矢島忠直が庄司として派遣され風間姓を名乗り、これが各地の[[風間氏]]の発祥となっている。 神社に伝わる「太々神楽獅子舞」は[[1822年]]（[[文政]]5年）から続く、歌詞がなく太刀を持つ雄獅子であり、[[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）に長野市[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html 風間神社太々神楽獅子舞] - 長野市文化財データベース。 == 歴史 == * [[596年]]（[[推古天皇]]5年）[[8月]] - [[日本書紀]]に記述が見える。 * [[860年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年）[[2月]] - [[日本三代実録]]に記述が見える。 * [[979年]]（[[天元 (日本)|天元]]2年） - [[諏訪氏]]の庶流・矢島忠直が庄司として派遣され、[[風間氏]]を名乗る。 * [[1697年]]（[[元禄]]10年） - 諏訪社から風間大明神に改称[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127　風間神社　〜式内社に改名した風神〜] - 週刊長野。 * [[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）[[8月1日]] - 「風間神社太々神楽獅子舞」が長野市[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に指定される。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]]・[[長野電鉄]][[長野駅]]から[[アルピコ交通]]（[[川中島バス]]）'''46'''系統に乗車、「風間」下車徒歩2分 == 周辺 == * 西風間公民館 * [[長野市役所]] 大豆島支所 * [[長野市立大豆島小学校]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[風間氏]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html 風間神社] - 長野県神社庁 * [http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ 風間神社太々神楽保存会] {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かさましんしや}} [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 風間神社 |画像 = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|風間神社（長野県長野市風間）]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[長野市]]大字[[風間 (長野市)|風間]]上風間779 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=風間神社}} |祭神 = [[伊勢津彦|伊勢津彦命]]
[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦命]]（主祭神）
[[事代主|事代主命]]
[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月1日]]・[[10月2日|2日]]（秋季例大祭） |神事 = }} '''風間神社'''（かざまじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]][[風間 (長野市)|風間]]にある[[神社]]。 [[式内社]]に比定される。 == 概要 == [[長野駅]]から南東約3kmほど、[[長野県道34号長野菅平線|県道]]からやや入った住宅地に鎮座する。 [[860年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年）「飄別神に叙位」との記述がある。[[979年]]（[[天元 (日本)|天元]]2年）には[[諏訪氏]]の庶流・矢島忠直が庄司として派遣され風間姓を名乗り、これが各地の[[風間氏]]の発祥となっている。 神社に伝わる「太々神楽獅子舞」は[[1822年]]（[[文政]]5年）から続く、歌詞がなく太刀を持つ雄獅子であり、[[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）に長野市[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択されている[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html 風間神社太々神楽獅子舞] - 長野市文化財データベース。 == 歴史 == * [[596年]]（[[推古天皇]]5年）[[8月]] - [[日本書紀]]に記述が見える。 * [[860年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年）[[2月]] - [[日本三代実録]]に記述が見える。 * [[979年]]（[[天元 (日本)|天元]]2年） - [[諏訪氏]]の庶流・矢島忠直が庄司として派遣され、[[風間氏]]を名乗る。 * [[1697年]]（[[元禄]]10年） - 諏訪社から風間大明神に改称[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127　風間神社　〜式内社に改名した風神〜] - 週刊長野。 * [[1822年]]（[[文政]]5年9月） - 神祇管領長[[吉田家]]により風間神社と改称。 * [[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）[[8月1日]] - 「風間神社太々神楽獅子舞」が長野市[[選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択される。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]]・[[長野電鉄]][[長野駅]]から[[アルピコ交通]]（[[川中島バス]]）'''46'''系統に乗車、「風間」下車徒歩2分 == 周辺 == * 西風間公民館 * [[長野市役所]] 大豆島支所 * [[長野市立大豆島小学校]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] == 関連項目 == * [[風間氏]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html 風間神社] - 長野県神社庁 * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=風間神社太々神楽保存会 |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かさましんしや}} [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]]

{{Shrine |名称 = Kazama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |所在地 = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |位置 = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main Deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind Deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}

The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind Deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}

{{ill|Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind Deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main Deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}

Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind Deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind Deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}

Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kazama Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year }}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5th year }} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year }} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year }}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10th year }}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year }} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year }} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kazama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:風間神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}

{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}

The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year }}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year }}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year }}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year }}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year }}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5th year }} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year }} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year }}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10th year }}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year }} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year }} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kazama Shrine}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=draft}}
