The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|12=draft}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|12=draft}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|12=draft}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|12=draft}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|12=draft}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|12=draft}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|12=draft}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|12=draft}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5th year|12=draft}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|12=draft}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|12=draft}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10th year|12=draft}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|12=draft}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|12=draft}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|12=draft}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|12=draft}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|12=draft}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日|12=draft}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日|12=draft}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|12=draft}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|12=draft}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|12=draft}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|12=draft}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|12=draft}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|12=draft}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|12=draft}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|12=draft}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5th year|12=draft}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|12=draft}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|12=draft}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10th year|12=draft}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|12=draft}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|12=draft}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|12=draft}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kazama Shrine}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|12=draft}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5th year|12=draft}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|12=draft}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|12=draft}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10th year|12=draft}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|12=draft}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|12=draft}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|12=draft}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5th year}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10th year}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kazama Shrine}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kazama Shrine}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City|qq=}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuhiko|qq=}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai-sha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 1|qq=}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 2|qq=}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City|qq=}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai-sha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Station|qq=}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa clan|qq=}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinai-sha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Station|qq=}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Nagano City]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Station|qq=}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City|qq=}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuhiko|qq=}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 1|qq=}} & {{ill|October 2|ja|10月2日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 2|qq=}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City|qq=}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Station|qq=}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa clan|qq=}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Station|qq=}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]]

{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small Shrine)

The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Station|qq=}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuhiko|qq=}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]]

The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuhiko|qq=}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[en:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]]

The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano�]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuhiko|qq=}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]]

The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1998年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998 CE|ja|平成10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1998年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢津彦}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1998 CE|ja||1998年|1=1998|2=ja|3=1998年|lt=Heisei 10|lt_ja=平成10年||qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1998年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Kazama Shrine]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]]

{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]]
