revision.text:
{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢津彦|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢津彦}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]]

Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = [[File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg|250px|Kazama Shrine (Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture)]] |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|0.6|N|138|13|12.2|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kazama Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|lt=Isetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|伊勢津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢津彦|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢津彦}}
{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|級長津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]]

The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223476]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223476]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.6335|N|138.220056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|qid=Q17193127}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Nagano|qid=Q128849}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223476]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223476]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.6335|N|138.220056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|qid=Q17193127}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Nagano|qid=Q128849}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]]

The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Nagano|qid=Q128849}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[sn:Shikinaisha]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[sn:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Nagano|qid=Q128849}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[sn:Shikinaisha]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[sn:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[sn:Shikinaisha]][[sn:Kazama Shrine]][[sn:Shikinaisha]] {{wikidata link|Q17223476}}

Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Nagano|qid=Q128849}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q17223476}}

The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|qq=|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q17223476}}

[[ja:風間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17223476]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17223476]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.6335|N|138.220056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|WD=Q17193127}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q17223476}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q17223476}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:風間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17223476]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17223476]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.6335|N|138.220056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|WD=Q17193127}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] & [[:en:October 2|October 2]] (Autumn Festival) |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kazama area of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]], in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]].[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * [[:en:1998|Heisei 10]] (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] }} [[ja:風間神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q17223476}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
