Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998|en|1998|WD=Q2089|lt=Heisei 10|1=1998}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:風間神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17223476}} [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:風間神社]] [[ja:風間神社]] [[ja:風間神社]] {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kazama Shrine |image = File:Kazama-jinja Shrine.jpg |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, Ōaza Kazama Kamikazama 779 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.6335|N|138.220056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Isetsuhiko|WD=Q17193127|1=Isetsuhiko}} |神体 = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Small Shrine)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |alternative names = |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|October 1|en|October 1|WD=Q2913|lt=October 1|1=October 1}} & {{ill|October 2|en|October 2|WD=Q2932|lt=October 2|1=October 2}} (Autumn Festival) |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Kazama Shrine''' (かざまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the Kazama area of {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated approximately 3 km southeast of {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}}, in a residential area slightly off the prefectural road {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Road 34|ja|長野県道34号長野菅平線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Road 34|1=Nagano Prefectural Road Route 34|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654596}}. Records indicate that in {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Hyoubetsu Shrine" was granted Rank in the court hierarchy. In {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tadanobu Yajima, a minor branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan|1=Suwa clan}}, was dispatched as a steward and took the surname Kazama, marking the origins of various {{ill|Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clan|lt=Kazama clans|ja|風間氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clans|qq=}}. The shrine preserves a traditional lion dance known as the "Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance," which has been performed since {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1998|en|1998|WD=Q2089|lt=Heisei 10|1=1998}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998|en|1998|WD=Q2089|lt=Heisei 10|1=1998}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:風間神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17223476}} [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

The dance features male lions wielding swords and no lyrics. It was designated a {{ill|Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|長野市選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1998|en|1998|qid=Q2089|lt=Heisei 10|1=1998}}.[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1166.html Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance] - Nagano City Cultural Heritage Database == History == * {{ill|596 CE|ja|推古天皇5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/596 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August): Mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. * {{ill|860 CE|ja|貞観2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/860 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February): Recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. * {{ill|979 CE|ja|天元2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/979 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Tadanobu Yajima of the Suwa clan was dispatched as a steward and adopted the name Kazama. * {{ill|1697 CE|ja|元禄10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1697 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Renamed from Suwa Shrine to Kazama Daimyōjin.[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2012/01/127.html 127 Kazama Shrine: The Wind deity Renamed as a Shikinaisha] - Weekly Nagano * {{ill|1822 CE|ja|文政5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1822 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (September): Renamed Kazama Shrine by the Yoshida clan, who served as shrine administrators. * {{ill|1998|en|1998|qid=Q2089|lt=Heisei 10|1=1998}} (August 1): The Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Lion Dance was designated as a Nagano City Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. == Access == * From {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|qid=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway), take the Alpico Kōtsū (Kawanakajima Bus) Route 46 to the "Kazama" stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk from the bus stop. == Nearby Facilities == * Nishikazama Community Center * Nagano City Office Dainoshima Branch * Nagano City Dainoshima Elementary School == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * ''Shrines and Temples of Shinshū'' (1981), Shinano Mainichi Shimbun == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kazama clan|ja|風間氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage3.nifty.com/nisikazama/ |title=Kazama Shrine Daidaikagura Preservation Society |date=20040712201316}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|風間神社|version=77203009|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q17223476|ja|風間神社}} [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
